module1.clean_text:
Rehospitalization form
The child [PATIENT] aged [AGE] originally from and residing in Algiers, from a 03rd degree consanguineous couple, followed since the age of [AGE] for a combined syndromic DIP type Ataxia telangectasia. Admitted today [DATE] for immunoglobulin treatment No. 04. HISTORY:
Family:
- Father: [AGE] years old; BES. - Mother: [AGE] years old, [ID] 3rd degree consanguinity. - Siblings:
Our Sick
Boy aged [AGE] with a history of a single kidney not investigated, RSP followed by a private pediatrician placed on GH since the age of [AGE] and for 6 [AGE] Boy aged [AGE] in BES
Boy died at the age of [AGE] (Cantrell Pentalogy)
Boy aged [AGE] with a history of hospitalization for 10 days for undocumented DR + adrenal insufficiency
Personal:
Physiological:
-Pregnancy: well monitored. -Delivery: by VB at term. - Apgar good at birth. 9/10
-Birth weight: 03 kg
- Calm neonatal period
-Exclusive breastfeeding for up to one month then artificial breastfeeding. -Dietary diversification: 06 [AGE] - vitamin D received at 1 month. - Vaccination: done up to [AGE] years old
Pathological:
-Delay in psychomotor acquisitions
-Repeated ear infections since the age of [AGE] allergic rhinitis
-Seizures since the age of [AGE] of illness:
Main diagnosis: Syndromic combined PID type Ataxia telangiectasia. Diagnostic elements:
Anamnestic: Concept of 3rd degree consanguinity
Concept of death among siblings at a young age
Concept of recurrent respiratory infections
Clinical: Static and kinetic ataxia
Bilateral telangiectasia
Paraclinical: Assessment of immunodeficiency done on [DATE] in favor of ataxia telangiectasia syndrome (Louis Bar syndrome)
Associated diagnosis: Pulmonary hamartochondroma/EPILEPSY/Right tympanic perforation
Date of diagnosis: [DATE] of diagnosis: [AGE] years. Hospitalization history:
Autoimmunity assessment: [DATE] Antinuclear Antibody Screening: Negative
Gastric tube result: negative direct examination and negative culture
Alpha fetoprotein 09/11/2025: 360.4 iu/mL
Processing in progress
Itraconazole 5mg/kg/day
Bactrim: 25mg/kg/day
Kepam: 220 mg *2/day
Therapeutic compliance: Good
Intermediate history:
Consultation: yes (Parnet Hospital)
On the respiratory level: 07/12/2025 Wet cough
On the digestive level: 11/29/2025 Acute gastroenteritis, treated with: ORS, Smecta, Augmentin (for 10 days)
On the oral level: 12/12/2025 Dental abscess treated with flagyl + clamoxyl
Neurologically: 11/12/2025 and 12/12/2025 2 convulsive attacks lasting 2 min made up of fixed gaze and tonic seizure of the upper limbs
On the genital level: Intervention for cryptorchidism cancelled. CLINICAL EXAMINATION UPON ADMISSION: Date: 15/12/2025
Anthropometric measurements:
Weight: 19 kg (-3.90 DS). Underweight
Size: 123CM (-3.29 DS). Marked stature delay
BMI: 13.8% (-2.9)
General condition preserved, afebrile: T: 36.6° C,
Skin exam:
Presence of cutaneous xerosis with slight white scales around the mouth
Telangiectasia in the right cheek
Dry dandruff on the hair
Pleuropulmonary examination:
Wet cough with slightly greenish sputum,
Eupneic, Fr: 20c/min, SpO2: 98% under ambient air
No respiratory distress
Lung auscultation: snoring rales with bronchial congestion
No signs of respiratory failure
Cardiovascular examination: HR: 89 b/min, CRT < 3 sec, Auscultation: B1, B2 audible properties, absence of added noises
Abdominopelvic examination: Soft abdomen, no organomegaly, free hernial orifices, preserved transit
Ophthalmological examination: Bilateral telangiectasia, rotatory nystagmus
ENT: no otorrhea
Examination of genitals: male morphotype, no ADS, TANNER stage 1, bilateral cryptorchidism
Osteoarticular examination:
Spine deformity,
Equinus foot deformity
Neurological examination:
Inability to walk (drunken walking): Kinetic ataxia
Impossibility of standing without support: Static ataxia
Word: Bradylalia, cerebellar dysarthria
Review of other devices: Is unremarkable
PARACLINICAL ASSESSMENT UPON ADMISSION: 15/12/25
CBC: GB: 4600 elements, PNN: 2900 element/mm lymphocytes: 1000 elements/mm
HB: 10.9g/dl; MCV: 82.1 fl;
CCMH: 34.8 Discrete normocytic normochromic anemia
PLQ: 327,000 el/mm 3. CRP: Between 12 and 24 mg/l
PCT: 0.05 ng/ml
PT: 14.3sec TP: 81.5% TCK: 34 sec Fibrinogen: 3.57 g/l
ASAT: 30.9U/l ALT: 21.6 U/L gGt: 25.15 U/l PAL: 261.8 U/l
What to do:
-Hospitalization
-Condition, peripheral venous route
- ECBC with mycological and parasitological study + antibiogram launched
-Launched a complete assessment
- Immunoglobulin treatment at 0.8 g/kg or 15g over 4 hours 30 min
-Education of the mother for respiratory physiotherapy
During the treatment: The immunoglobulin treatment passed without incident,
The child is discharged on 12/16/2025 with a check-up appointment: 01/18/2026. CONCLUSION:
The child Maabed Mouad aged 12, originally from and residing in Algiers, from a 3rd degree consanguineous couple, followed for a syndromic combined DIP type Ataxia telangiectasia, Admitted to our level for his treatment of immunoglobulin No. 04. To collect / To do:
Discharge the patient with: Clamoxyl tab 01g every 8 hours (PDT 10 days) + Zomax tab 500mg/day (PDT 5 days)
Retrieval of ECBC results + stool coproparasitology
Patient scheduled for tooth extraction on 16/12/2025
Recover the level of residual IgG during the next treatment
Appointment for the 5th Immunoglobulin treatment on 01/18/2026
Department resident: Dr Djoudi Department assistant: Dr Doukar

inconsistencies.message: Female-specific term 'pregnancy' found in male patient record

phenopacket.input:
Rehospitalization form
The child Maabed Mouad aged 12, originally from and residing in Algiers, from a 03rd degree consanguineous couple, followed since the age of 08, for a combined syndromic DIP type Ataxia telangectasia. Admitted today 12/15/2025 for immunoglobulin treatment No. 04. HISTORY:
Family:
- Father: 41 years old; BES. - Mother: 33 years old, G8P04A03M01
- 3rd degree consanguinity. - Siblings:
Our Sick
Boy aged 10, with a history of a single kidney not investigated, RSP followed by a private pediatrician placed on GH since the age of 3 and for 6 months. Boy aged 08 in BES
Boy died at the age of 2 months (Cantrell Pentalogy)
Boy aged 13 months with a history of hospitalization for 10 days for undocumented DR + adrenal insufficiency
Personal:
Physiological:
-Pregnancy: well monitored. -Delivery: by VB at term. - Apgar good at birth. 9/10
-Birth weight: 03 kg
- Calm neonatal period
-Exclusive breastfeeding for up to one month then artificial breastfeeding. -Dietary diversification: 06 months. - vitamin D received at 1 month. - Vaccination: done up to 6 years old
Pathological:
-Delay in psychomotor acquisitions
-Repeated ear infections since the age of 3
-Recurrent allergic rhinitis
-Seizures since the age of 4
History of illness:
Main diagnosis: Syndromic combined PID type Ataxia telangiectasia. Diagnostic elements:
Anamnestic: Concept of 3rd degree consanguinity
Concept of death among siblings at a young age
Concept of recurrent respiratory infections
Clinical: Static and kinetic ataxia
Bilateral telangiectasia
Paraclinical: Assessment of immunodeficiency done on 05/22/2025: in favor of ataxia telangiectasia syndrome (Louis Bar syndrome)
Associated diagnosis: Pulmonary hamartochondroma/EPILEPSY/Right tympanic perforation
Date of diagnosis: 2021
Age of diagnosis: 08 years. Hospitalization history:
Autoimmunity assessment: 11/30/2025 Antinuclear Antibody Screening: Negative
Gastric tube result: negative direct examination and negative culture
Alpha fetoprotein 09/11/2025: 360.4 iu/mL
Processing in progress
Itraconazole 5mg/kg/day
Bactrim: 25mg/kg/day
Kepam: 220 mg *2/day
Therapeutic compliance: Good
Intermediate history:
Consultation: yes (Parnet Hospital)
On the respiratory level: 07/12/2025 Wet cough
On the digestive level: 11/29/2025 Acute gastroenteritis, treated with: ORS, Smecta, Augmentin (for 10 days)
On the oral level: 12/12/2025 Dental abscess treated with flagyl + clamoxyl
Neurologically: 11/12/2025 and 12/12/2025 2 convulsive attacks lasting 2 min made up of fixed gaze and tonic seizure of the upper limbs
On the genital level: Intervention for cryptorchidism cancelled. CLINICAL EXAMINATION UPON ADMISSION: Date: 15/12/2025
Anthropometric measurements:
Weight: 19 kg (-3.90 DS). Underweight
Size: 123CM (-3.29 DS). Marked stature delay
BMI: 13.8% (-2.9)
General condition preserved, afebrile: T: 36.6° C,
Skin exam:
Presence of cutaneous xerosis with slight white scales around the mouth
Telangiectasia in the right cheek
Dry dandruff on the hair
Pleuropulmonary examination:
Wet cough with slightly greenish sputum,
Eupneic, Fr: 20c/min, SpO2: 98% under ambient air
No respiratory distress
Lung auscultation: snoring rales with bronchial congestion
No signs of respiratory failure
Cardiovascular examination: HR: 89 b/min, CRT < 3 sec, Auscultation: B1, B2 audible properties, absence of added noises
Abdominopelvic examination: Soft abdomen, no organomegaly, free hernial orifices, preserved transit
Ophthalmological examination: Bilateral telangiectasia, rotatory nystagmus
ENT: no otorrhea
Examination of genitals: male morphotype, no ADS, TANNER stage 1, bilateral cryptorchidism
Osteoarticular examination:
Spine deformity,
Equinus foot deformity
Neurological examination:
Inability to walk (drunken walking): Kinetic ataxia
Impossibility of standing without support: Static ataxia
Word: Bradylalia, cerebellar dysarthria
Review of other devices: Is unremarkable
PARACLINICAL ASSESSMENT UPON ADMISSION: 15/12/25
CBC: GB: 4600 elements, PNN: 2900 element/mm lymphocytes: 1000 elements/mm
HB: 10.9g/dl; MCV: 82.1 fl;
CCMH: 34.8 Discrete normocytic normochromic anemia
PLQ: 327,000 el/mm 3. CRP: Between 12 and 24 mg/l
PCT: 0.05 ng/ml
PT: 14.3sec TP: 81.5% TCK: 34 sec Fibrinogen: 3.57 g/l
ASAT: 30.9U/l ALT: 21.6 U/L gGt: 25.15 U/l PAL: 261.8 U/l
What to do:
-Hospitalization
-Condition, peripheral venous route
- ECBC with mycological and parasitological study + antibiogram launched
-Launched a complete assessment
- Immunoglobulin treatment at 0.8 g/kg or 15g over 4 hours 30 min
-Education of the mother for respiratory physiotherapy
During the treatment: The immunoglobulin treatment passed without incident,
The child is discharged on 12/16/2025 with a check-up appointment: 01/18/2026. CONCLUSION:
The child Maabed Mouad aged 12, originally from and residing in Algiers, from a 3rd degree consanguineous couple, followed for a syndromic combined DIP type Ataxia telangiectasia, Admitted to our level for his treatment of immunoglobulin No. 04. To collect / To do:
Discharge the patient with: Clamoxyl tab 01g every 8 hours (PDT 10 days) + Zomax tab 500mg/day (PDT 5 days)
Retrieval of ECBC results + stool coproparasitology
Patient scheduled for tooth extraction on 16/12/2025
Recover the level of residual IgG during the next treatment
Appointment for the 5th Immunoglobulin treatment on 01/18/2026
Department resident: Dr Djoudi Department assistant: Dr Doukar
